# Build Timeline — First Experiment → Final Product

**Date:** 2026-07-20 · **Scope:** the complete critical path, apparatus → measurement → (conditional) craft, with honest go/no-go gates.
**Grounding:** `snr_vs_quality.py`, `apparatus_artifact_budget.py`, the 7 subsystem memos, `phase1-apparatus-master-design`, `test-parameters-and-build-strategy`.
**Visual:** `Build-Timeline-Gantt.pdf` (generated by `build_timeline_gantt.py`).

> **Read this first — the shape of the plan.** This is NOT a straight march to a working drive. It is a **staged bet with kill-gates**: each phase is cheap-enough and decisive-enough that a *null* result ends the program there, having produced a publishable result. The honest expectation (momentum conservation + the Tajmar nulls) is that **the program terminates at Gate B or C with a clean null** — and that is a full success. Only a *positive Q-scaling signal that survives every discriminator* unlocks the next phase. Each gate is a real branch, not a formality.

---

## The five phases at a glance

| Phase | What you build | Duration | Cost (cum.) | Gate at end | If NULL → |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| **0. Apparatus + shakedown** | Rig + $5 lifter known-answer test | 6–9 mo | ~$40k | **Gate A: rig validated?** | fix rig, not physics |
| **1. Room-temp copper** | Frustum + pillbox null, F∝Q at 300 K | 6–9 mo | ~$45k | **Gate B: signal or bound?** | **publish the bound → STOP** |
| **2. Cryo-copper** | Cryostat, Q~10⁵, wider Q-sweep | 9–12 mo | ~$110k | **Gate C: F∝Q slope real?** | **publish tighter bound → STOP** |
| **3. Superconducting** | Nb₃Sn @ 4 K, Q~10¹⁰, confirmation | 12–18 mo | ~$200k | **Gate D: independently replicated?** | extraordinary-null → STOP |
| **4. Engineering / craft** | Multi-cell propulsion demonstrator | 3–10 yr | \$M+ | Gate E: thrust-to-weight | (only if Gates B–D all positive) |

**Total to a decisive physics answer (Gates B–C): ~2–2.5 years, ~$110k.** Phases 3–4 are *conditional* and only exist in the ~1% world where the effect is real.

---

## Phase 0 — Apparatus build + known-answer shakedown  (Months 0–9)
**Goal:** build the measurement chain and prove it works against a *known answer* before trusting it on the real article.

**Device detail — what you're building:**
- **Torsion balance:** 0.90 mm tungsten fibre × 300 mm, CFRP arm, cavity + counterweight at ±15 cm (κ≈3.5×10⁻² N·m/rad; 12 µN → 7.8 µm tip). Counterbalanced so the hot mass sits at ~zero moment arm (thermal-drift rejection). In-vacuum 180° rotation stage.
- **Readout:** optical lever (laser + position-sensitive detector) — ~1000× headroom on the 8 µm signal — architected for a later interferometer drop-in.
- **Vacuum:** used turbo (Pfeiffer HiPace 80 / Agilent TwisTorr 74) + dry backing pump, ≤10⁻⁶ torr, bellows-decoupled; wide-range Pirani+cold-cathode gauge logged continuously.
- **RF chain:** Valon 5015 synth → 10 W PA → isolator+circulator → **fixed off-balance thermal dump** → dual directional coupler → 2× traceable RMS sensors.
- **Control/DAQ:** dither/reflected-min PLL lock (Red Pitaya); Arduino square-waves the drive at ~0.02 Hz with blind-randomized scheduling; Python software lock-in.
- **EM/thermal:** mu-metal shield, coaxial zero-loop feed leads, star ground, fiber-optic temp sensors inside the RF field + PT100s off-field.

**Milestones:** procure (rig + used vacuum) → assemble balance + optical lever → commission vacuum to 10⁻⁶ torr → integrate RF + PLL lock → **build the ~$5 Biefeld–Brown lifter**.

> ### ▸ GATE A — "Does the apparatus tell the truth?"
> Run the **lifter**: it must **lift in air and read ZERO in vacuum** (known answer). Also: electrostatic + photon-pressure calibrations agree <2%; pillbox reads zero.
> - **PASS** (rig reproduces the known answer) → Phase 1.
> - **FAIL** (rig shows thrust in vacuum, or calibrations disagree) → **the rig is broken, not physics.** Debug vacuum / thermal / EM until the lifter reads zero. *Do not proceed on an unvalidated rig — this is the single most important discipline in the whole program.*

## Phase 1 — Room-temperature copper campaign  (Months 9–18)
**Goal:** the first real F∝Q data, at Q~2.7×10⁴.

**Device detail:** the machined **OFHC copper frustum** (Ø160→Ø90, mirror bore) + the matched **symmetric pillbox null-control**, per `HD-CU-CAV-001`. TM010 ≈ 1.836 GHz, Q₀≈27,000.

**Milestones:** machine frustum + pillbox → RF-commission (find TM010, measure Q on a VNA) → run the modulated campaign at **constant dissipated power**, interleaving the pillbox null + the full reversal matrix (180° rotation, current-polarity, pressure sweep) each session → regress result vs measured temperatures.

> ### ▸ GATE B — "Signal, or a bound?"  ★ the most likely program terminus
> - **NULL** (no F∝Q above floor; SNR was marginal ~2–22 here anyway) → **write the paper: a bound on anomalous thrust + the Q-scaling methodology. PUBLISH. STOP.** This is a real, citable result and a complete, honest end to the program.
> - **CANDIDATE SIGNAL** (a force tracking Q that survives all reversals) → the room-temp SNR is too marginal to trust alone → **proceed to Phase 2 to confirm with a bigger Q lever.**

## Phase 2 — Cryo-copper campaign  (Months 18–30)
**Goal:** raise Q to ~10⁵ (confident SNR 8–80) and extend the F∝Q slope — the first *slope* across a real Q range.

**Device detail:** add a **closed-cycle cryostat** (pulse-tube, ~4 K) with vibration isolation (the cryo path's *new* enemy). The cryostat **cryopumps to ultra-high vacuum for free** (retires the separate turbo). Cavity → **high-RRR copper** so cooling actually buys Q. Same balance, now cryo-compatible.

**Milestones:** integrate cryostat + isolation → cool down, re-measure Q at 300 K and 4 K (confirm the ~4× lever) → repeat the full campaign at 4 K → fit **F vs Q across 300 K + 4 K points** (the actual discriminator).

> ### ▸ GATE C — "Is the F∝Q slope real?"
> - **NULL / slope consistent with zero** → **publish the tighter bound (SNR up to ~80). STOP.** A stronger result than Phase 1's.
> - **SLOPE PRESENT** (force scales with Q, artifacts flat, pillbox zero) → genuinely surprising → **proceed to Phase 3 for the unambiguous superconducting confirmation.**

## Phase 3 — Superconducting confirmation  (Months 30–48)
**Goal:** the unambiguous test at Q~10¹⁰ (SNR ~10⁶) — mitigation-irrelevant.

**Device detail:** **Nb₃Sn cavity at 4.2 K** (same cryostat; ordinary liquid He, not superfluid). Fabrication/coating via **SRF collaboration** (JLab / Fermilab / Cornell / TRIUMF). Everything else carries over.

**Milestones:** secure SRF partner → coat/acquire Nb₃Sn frustum + pillbox → cold Q-verification (~10¹⁰) → final campaign → **the three-rung F∝Q fit (Cu → cryo-Cu → Nb₃Sn)** → prepare for independent replication.

> ### ▸ GATE D — "Independently replicated?"
> - Any positive result here is **extraordinary** and means nothing until an *independent group* (ideally Tajmar's) reproduces it end-to-end. **Publish + hand the full reproducible package to independent labs.**
> - **Replicated** → only now is the physics real. → Phase 4.
> - **Not replicated / null on replication** → the earlier signal was a subtle systematic; report it honestly. STOP.

## Phase 4 — Engineering / the craft  (Years, conditional, \$M+)
**Only enters the world if Gates B–D are ALL positive AND independently replicated.**
- Scale from a single high-Q cell to a **multi-cell array** for usable thrust-to-weight; solve the *real* engineering (cryogenics at scale, power, structure, the thermodynamic-vise waste-heat problem).
- This is a multi-year, multi-\$M, institutional/industrial program — a *different kind of project* from the physics test. The horizon-drive "craft" design docs describe *this* endpoint; they are the **destination, not the near-term plan.**

---

## The honest critical path (what actually decides everything)
1. **Gate A is the linchpin** — an unvalidated rig makes every later result worthless. The lifter's "zero in vacuum" is the cheapest, most important measurement in the program.
2. **Gate B is the most likely ending** — a published bound + methodology, ~2 years and ~$45k in. Plan for this as the realistic outcome; treat everything past it as low-probability upside.
3. **The Q-ladder IS the experiment** — each phase exists to add one more point to the F∝Q fit. Power and size don't help; only Q (temperature) does.
4. **Every gate is a real branch.** The discipline that makes this credible science (and a defensible PhD) is the willingness to STOP and publish a null at each gate rather than chase a signal that isn't scaling with Q.

## Timeline realism note
Durations assume a resourced university/lab setting (the only viable context — see the cost analysis). A part-time or under-funded effort stretches each phase 1.5–2×. Cryo integration (Phase 2) and SRF partnership (Phase 3) are the two schedule risks; both are *external-dependency* risks, not technical unknowns.

*All numbers reproduce from the package harness (`verify_all.py`, 19 checks). This timeline is a planning artifact; real durations/costs co-refine with the host group.*
